# Scout / Explorer Agent

#### 2026/4/21

**Distribution of search calls (0 means direct answer without tool use)**

1. Agent often directly answers with “Full Trajectory”  
2. With “Selected Tools”, using the “Original Message” format, the model actually issues moderate number of search calls (in between “Full Trajectory” and “Summary”)

![][image1]  
**Distribution of indices of “selected tool calls”**

- First call is selected pretty often  
- First 5 calls takes up 40% of the selected calls   
- Still \> 10% of total selected calls coming from more than 20 steps. 

![][image2]

#### 2026/4/14

##### Execute conditioned on selected tool calls

Recap Setting from Previous Weeks:

1. Two templates for converting trajectories into strings  
   1. **Tags:** Add \[reasoning\], \[tool call\], \[tool outputs\] tags   
   2. **Original Messages:** Dump the original messages (raw JSON) produced by the model  
   3. All these trajectories are converted into plain text  
   4. How are the selected tool calls formatted as input to the second round? 

Original search agent:  
\<think\> \</think\>  
\<search\> \</search\>  
\<info\> \</info\>

Formatting the explorer output to mitigate “domain shift” as if they are the first five steps of the search agent itself.

- Issue: out-of-order?  
  - We could potentially “rewrite” them to be more continuous?

2. Truncation settings: (to avoid going over context limit of gpt-oss)  
   1. Max length of whole trajectory: 500,000 characters  
   2. Max length of reasoning: 3000 characters  
   3. Max length of tool outputs (retrieval): 5000 characters  
   4. (Can compute how much is truncated)  
3. Baselines:   
   1. Full trajectory as input  
   2. Summary of trajectory as input:   
      1. Full trajectory as input to the summarizer   
      2. Second round takes as input the summary

Issue: Getting the following error when using full trajectory, with original messages

- Error: Error code: 400 \- {'error': {'message': 'Unknown channel: comment', 'type': 'BadRequestError', 'param': None, 'code': 400}}

**New Baseline: k(=5) selected tools from trajectory as input, selected by gemini**  
Setting

1. Tool call selection model: gemini-2.5-pro  
2. Out of all search calls, select 5 search calls based on index. The prompt directly specifies which (index, search call) pairs are available and let gemini select 5 from the set.   
   1. It could hallucinate in the current setting.  
   2. Invalid indices rate:   
      1. Tags: 0% \=\> all valid, no hallucination  
      2. Original Messages: 0.6%  
3. Format the input prompt by (exact input prompt can be found in the “Selected Tool Calls” column in the [visualizer](https://huggingface.co/spaces/timchen0618/dashboard))   
   1. Take the selected search call and retrieval outputs  
   2. Take all the preceding reasoning texts, up until the previous search call. (There could be many reasoning in between search calls for gpt-oss-120b)

##### 

Results on BrowseComp Test set (N=830)

1. [Raw outputs](https://huggingface.co/spaces/timchen0618/dashboard%20) (Tab Visualizer \-\> Selected Tools)   
2. Accuracy on bcp dataset (row 4, bolded)    
   1. Note: the “Original Messages” results are not missing 20 examples. 

|  | GPT-OSS-120B \+ Qwen3-8B-Embedding | Inject at Start |  |  |
| :---- | :---- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
|  |  | Acc | Recall | \# calls |
| Base (No Plan) | Base agent  | 43.6 | 53.4 | 19.8 |
| **Template: Tags** |  |  |  |  |
| Base (No Plan) | Second run takes the output from first run, full trajectory | 47.4 | 15.0 | 4.4 |
| Base (Summary) | Second run takes the **summary** of first run trajectory and do the task again | 46.8 | 54.8 | 18.1 |
| **Base (No plan)** | **Second run takes the selected output of 5 search calls from the first run** | 46.3 | 12.1 | 4.9 |
| **Original Messages** |  |  |  |  |
| Base (No Plan) | Second run takes the output from first run, full trajectory | 43.2 | 18.6 | 5.5 |
| Base (Summary) | Second run takes the **summary** of first run trajectory and do the task again | 47.5 | 55.7 | 17.8 |
| **Base (No plan)** | **Second run takes the selected output of 5 search calls from the first run** | 50.2 | 38.9 | 13.3 |
| Base (A bit of oracle) | Best of N (N 5 subsets of search calls from first run) |  |  |  |
|  | retrospective | 47.2 | 58.2 | 19.3 |
|  | Best Planning Performance So Far | 45.9 | 59.2 | 25.5 |

Discussion / Observations

- Summary actually performs quite well on the full dataset   
- With “5 selected calls”, gains are slightly smaller but fewer number of calls (\~5)   
  - In the second round, the agent “directly answers” a lot of the questions (67%)  
  - Below is a split between queries where the agent “directly answers” or “does searches”   
    \=\> queries that require more searches are harder, and we see more gain on those searches   
- One weird behavior to note for run with “5 selected calls” is that gpt-oss-120b often generates “fake tool calls”, like “\[Tool call\]”, “\[Tool results\]”, etc.   
  - \=\> This is solved by passing the original messages format as input. 

Split the queries by whether the second round agent did more searches or directly answers

| Cluster | N | \# \# Base (First round) Correct | Base (First round) Acc | \# SelTools Correct | SelTools Acc | Δ |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| A (direct answer) | 560 | 291 | 52.0% | 298 | 53.2% | \+1.2pp |
| B (did searches) | 270 | 59 | 21.9% | 86 | 31.9% | \+10.0pp |
| **Overall** | **830** | **350** | **42.2%** | **384** | **46.3%** | **\+4.1pp** |

Out of 560 where second round agent directly answers:   
   ┌───────────────────────┬───────┬───────┐                                                                                                                                                                                             
   │        Outcome           │ Count │   %   │                                                                                                                                                                                             
   ├───────────────────────┼───────┼───────┤                                                                                                                                                                                             
   │Same answer           │ 235   │ **42.0%** │                                                                                                                                                                                             
   ├───────────────────────┼───────┼───────┤                                                                                                                                                                                             
   │**Changed (any)**        │ **325**   │ **58.0%** │                                                                                                                                                                                             
   ├───────────────────────┼───────┼───────┤  
   │— ✗→✓ improved      │ 15    │ 2.7%  │                                                                                                                                                                                             
   ├───────────────────────┼───────┼───────┤                   
   │ — ✓→✗ regressed    │ 9      │ 1.6%  │  
   ├───────────────────────┼───────┼───────┤  
   │—✗→✗ different wrong │ 301 │ **53.8%** │                                                                                                                                                                                             
   └───────────────────────┴───────┴───────┘   

Analyzing selected tool calls

1. What is the distribution?  
2. % of empty indices. 

Next Steps:   
This project envision model 1 (scout / explorer) and model 2 (full task completion search agent):

2. Setup evaluation for other tasks?   
   1.   
3. Prompt the agent model to perform as explorer, see lower bound.   
4. **Train an explorer model**  
   1. We split BrowseComp data into train / dev / test?   
   2. Input: Query  
   3. Output: N search calls (N=5?)  
   4. Training data:   
      1.  On gemini trajectories    
   5. Base model (to be fine-tuning):

   6. Test in-domain performance   
   7. Train on 680 instances, test on 150 examples.   
5.   
6. Train a search agent given fixed explorer model  
   1. Just use the gemini for now to do the top 5 selection  
   2. Train the search agent as how others do.  
7. Leave one out search call selection?  
8. 

 

9. Find a training setting we can RL on   
   1. [SearchGym](https://github.com/JIA-Lab-research/SearchGym)?  
10. Potential training dataset:  
    1. 

#### 2026/4/8

##### Execute conditioned on previous trajectories

Recap: 

- Retrospectively generating a plan from agent trajectories, and then executing based on the generated plan yields some gain over the baseline.   
- What if we directly condition on one complete trajectory, or a summary of the trajectory? 

Setting: 

- Before execution, the agent sees either (1) A full trajectory on the same query (by the same agent) or (2) a summary of that trajectory

Results (on the first 50 examples of Browsecomp-Plus)   
[Planning Experiments](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/19zNflfnOQqi2oh639NcmYpZoh9-yT_SZjIu4cxIi8rY/edit?pli=1&gid=1943296468#gid=1943296468) (BCP first50)

**Training an explorer/scout (go out and try a few things to learn more about the environment) that searches for a few steps:**

* Setting:  
  * Explorer model:   
    * Gemini for generating training data  
    * Qwen (?) for training? Or Tongyi…   
  * Search Agent model:   
    * gpt-oss-120B?  
    * tongyi  
* Train an explorer model to search for a fixed number of calls (small N=1 / 5\)  
  * Then prepend the trajectory to the search agent (freezed), to get the reward?   
  * Quicker way to try: either SFT (rejection sampling) or DPO so we don’t need to calculate this for every step.   
    * We can also train reward models with the preference pairs, etc.      
  * Generating traces for explorer model  
    * Give gemini the entire trajectory, ask it to select 5 calls from it as “t\_init”  
    * Prepend “t\_init” to the search agent  
    * SFT explorer on “t\_init”  
* The hope is that this will teach the explorer model to search “efficiently”

Observation / Discussion 

1. Taking results from full trajectory does seem to help   
2. Starting from summary doesn’t really help; should look further into why it didn’t work as well   
   1. Sometimes gpt-oss-120b outputs the answer directly without summarizing the trajectory  
3. Also should run it on full set.   
4. Potential direction:   
   1. Could use a subagent which can also search and reason, and the final output is a structured high-level plan.   
   2. Get to row 5 without incurring so many calls in the first run.   
      1. Maybe have a iteration limit on the first run 

#### 

#### 2026/3/31

**Retrospectively generate plans from base agent trajectories, and execute using these plans**

- Step 1: Regular inference with gpt-oss → accuracy is 54?  
- Step 2: Gemini takes the output from Step 1, generates plan  
- Step 3: Do new inference with output from Step 2  
  - Accuracy is 62?  
-   
- Plan generation model: **gemini-2.5-pro**  
- Base agent: gpt-oss-120b

Results (On 50 Examples) [Subagent Experiments](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/19zNflfnOQqi2oh639NcmYpZoh9-yT_SZjIu4cxIi8rY/edit?gid=1943296468#gid=1943296468) [https://planviewer.streamlit.app/](https://planviewer.streamlit.app/) 

1. Planning doesn’t really help (doesn’t hurt either)  
2. Re-injecting seems to help for majority of cases, especially when the plan is more informative  
   1. Not sure why it doesn’t help for the retrospective case  
3. Retrospective plan helps, not sure if it is because of the explicit queries listed. 

8 examples where retrospective plan \> base agent. 

| QID | Answer | Key entity named in plan | first search in retro plan | Base searches | Retro searches |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 771 | Vakkorama | Vitali Hakko (step 4\) | **`Vitali Hakko`** `born wooden house railway father...` | 27 | 15 |
| 787 | Emmanuel Kwesi Danso Arthur Junior | Fireboy DML (step 3\) | **`Fireboy DML`** `first EP release year` | 32 | 21 |
| 792 | Oral creatine supplementation... | SpringerOpen (step 1\) | `publishing group launched in 2010 SpringerOpen` | 26 | 19 |
| 798 | Lady Shri Ram College for Women | Lady Shri Ram College (step 2\) | **`Lady Shri Ram College`** `former minister alumni` | 28 | 24 |
| 819 | Energetic; melodic; banging; clubby; bacon. | Chris Schweizer (step 4, after ruling out Armin van Buuren) | **`Chris Schweizer`** `math teacher DJ interview...` | 18 | 9 |
| 821 | 94% | "94% would recommend as a place to work" (step 6\) | `"sues" "rescinded" "job offer" 2021 "updated" "29 minutes"` | 30 | 9 |
| 834 | Cozens Ranch Museum | Cozens Ranch Museum (step 2\) | **`Cozens Ranch Museum`** `National Register...` | 27 | 14 |
| 836 | Tina Tutkova | Phil Wang → Avalon (step 2\) | **`Avalon`** `talent management founder showrunner` | 30 | 16 |
